ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general fish sandwich concept (FOODON_00002764); no specific Filet-O-Fish meal combo entry exists. Closest parent match used for the sandwich component.

ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT food concept for a fast-food fish burger meal combo. Not assigned to avoid fabrication.

serving.description: 1 meal set (Filet-O-Fish sandwich + corn + 500 ml bottled water)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard McDonald's Singapore Filet-O-Fish Meal set comprising one Filet-O-Fish sandwich (approximately 142 g), one corn on the cob side (approximately 100 g), and one 500 ml bottle of Ice Mountain mineral water (approximately 500 g water, negligible caloric contribution), totalling approximately 340 g of caloric food weight.

nutrient_source.title: McDonald's Singapore Nutrition Information — Filet-O-Fish and Corn; USDA FoodData Central — Corn on the cob (boiled); Ice Mountain mineral water (zero caloric contribution)
nutrient_source.organization: McDonald's Singapore (HPB Healthier Choice Programme data); USDA FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values for the Filet-O-Fish sandwich are derived from McDonald's Singapore published nutrition data (aligned with HPB Healthier Choice Programme listing). Corn on the cob values are from USDA FoodData Central (boiled yellow corn, FDC ID 170108). Ice Mountain bottled water contributes zero calories, protein, fat, or carbohydrate. Per-100g values are calculated as a weighted composite of the sandwich (142 g) and corn (100 g) components, excluding the water bottle weight from caloric calculations. Total caloric food weight used for per-100g calculation: 242 g.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — white bread / refined wheat bun analogous estimate; Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the McDonald's Filet-O-Fish Meal combo. GI estimated as a weighted composite: the steamed wheat bun component is analogous to a white bread / soft roll (GI ~70–72, University of Sydney GI Database); the corn on the cob component has a published GI of approximately 52 (sweet corn, University of Sydney GI Database, Atkinson et al. 2021). Weighted composite GI calculated as approximately 66 (medium). Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × available carbohydrate per serving in grams) / 100 = (66 × 47.9 g carbohydrate from 242 g food) / 100 ≈ 13.3.

hypertension.guidance: The Filet-O-Fish Meal provides approximately 720 mg sodium per serving (nearly 31% of the 2,300 mg daily limit recommended by the Singapore Health Promotion Board), primarily from the tartar sauce, processed cheese, and seasoned fish fillet; individuals with hypertension should limit frequency of consumption and avoid adding additional condiments.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Refined carbohydrate and medium glycemic index
type2_diabetes.guidance: The meal's steamed wheat bun contributes refined carbohydrates with a medium composite GI of approximately 66, producing a glycemic load of approximately 13 per serving; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should monitor portion size, pair with a non-starchy vegetable side where possible, and account for this meal within their total daily carbohydrate budget.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat, trans fat, and sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The deep-fried fish fillet and processed cheese contribute saturated fat (approximately 5 g per serving) and trace trans fat; combined with the high sodium load, individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should limit this meal to occasional consumption and choose the corn side over fries to reduce overall saturated fat and caloric density.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The McDonald's Filet-O-Fish Meal with Corn and Ice Mountain is a fast-food combo meal available at McDonald's Singapore restaurants, listed under the HPB Healthier Choice Programme as a sub-500-calorie option. The meal consists of a Filet-O-Fish sandwich — a deep-fried breaded Alaskan pollock fillet topped with a half-slice of processed cheese and tartar sauce, served in a soft steamed wheat bun — accompanied by a boiled corn on the cob side and a 500 ml bottle of Ice Mountain mineral water. The sandwich delivers a mild, savoury flavour with a crispy exterior from the breaded fillet, a creamy tang from the tartar sauce, and a soft, slightly sweet bun. The corn side adds natural sweetness, dietary fibre, and potassium. The water contributes no calories. Nutritionally, the meal provides approximately 424 kcal per serving (based on 242 g caloric food weight), with moderate protein (~20 g), moderate fat (~18 g), and approximately 48 g of carbohydrate. Sodium is a key concern at approximately 720 mg per serving. The composite glycemic index is estimated at 66 (medium), making it a moderate glycemic meal. The meal is certified Halal by MUIS Singapore. It is suitable for occasional consumption by individuals managing weight, but sodium and refined carbohydrate content require monitoring for those with hypertension or Type 2 Diabetes. The Filet-O-Fish sandwich has been a McDonald's menu staple since 1962 and is one of the most widely recognised fast-food fish products globally.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from McDonald's Singapore published nutrition data (aligned with HPB Healthier Choice Programme) and USDA FoodData Central for the corn component. The per-100g values are calculated composites across the sandwich and corn components. No direct laboratory analysis of this specific meal combination was available. GI is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database values for white bread/soft roll and sweet corn. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. The meal name references 'Ice Mountain' which is a brand of bottled mineral water (Nestlé Ice Mountain); it contributes zero calories, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sodium, or other nutrients to the per-100g calculation. The water bottle weight (500 g) is excluded from the caloric food weight denominator (242 g = 142 g sandwich + 100 g corn). 2. Per-100g values are calculated as a weighted composite of the Filet-O-Fish sandwich (142 g, ~330 kcal per McDonald's Singapore nutrition data) and boiled corn on the cob (100 g, ~96 kcal per USDA FDC ID 170108), divided by 242 g total caloric food weight. 3. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the McDonald's Singapore nutrition panel or USDA composite used for this meal. 4. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the McDonald's Singapore nutrition panel for this meal. 5. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the McDonald's Singapore nutrition panel for this meal. 6. The food_name has been normalised to 'Filet-O-Fish Meal with Corn and Bottled Water' as the canonical English name; 'Ice Mountain' is a brand name retained in aliases and the original field survey name. 7. GI is an analogous estimate (composite ~66, medium) based on University of Sydney GI Database values for soft wheat roll (~70) and sweet corn (~52), weighted by carbohydrate contribution. 8. The meal is listed under McDonald's Singapore HPB Healthier Choice Programme as under 500 calories, consistent with the input description. 9. The Tamil alias in aliases_by_language and flat aliases has been corrected to pure Tamil script: ஃபிலட்-ஓ-ஃபிஷ் உணவு சோளம் மற்றும் கனிம நீருடன் — a mixed-script corruption (Cyrillic characters) present in the previous iteration has been removed. All aliases_by_language entries have been verified to appear verbatim in the flat aliases list.
